title: Publisher Admission, Buffering, and Backpressure · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs publisher admission, buffering, and backpressure from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a cancellable Go publisher admission controller after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat a nil PublishWithContext error as local call acceptance only and separately track flow control, routing, confirmation, and stakeholder effect.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Blocked Unblocked Flow
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 7984 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; retain screen-reader-readable topology and delivery evidence; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Blocked Unblocked Flow trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Blocked Unblocked Flow.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Blocked Unblocked Flow, keep Blocked Unblocked Flow connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Publish Context Admission, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A nil PublishWithContext error proves RabbitMQ durably accepted and routed the publication.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to propagate broker blocking, byte capacity, and confirm-window saturation into admission; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Respond to blocked and unblocked connection events, broker flow control, alarms, and local drain evidence without confusing their scopes; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Blocked Unblocked Flow, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A drain event proves RabbitMQ resource alarms and queue backlog have cleared. In Go, PublishWithContext is not proof that broker I/O stopped when its context ended.
